description: ClusterKeycloak is the Schema for the clusterkeycloaks API.

apiVersion.description:
APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.
Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and
may reject unrecognized values.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources

kind.description:
Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.
Servers may infer this from the endpoint the client submits requests to.
Cannot be updated.
In CamelCase.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds

spec.description: ClusterKeycloakSpec defines the desired state of ClusterKeycloak.

adminType.description:
AdminType can be user or serviceAccount, if serviceAccount was specified,
then client_credentials grant type should be used for getting admin realm token.
Deprecated: Use Auth instead. When Auth is set, this field is ignored.

auth.description:
Auth defines the authentication configuration for connecting to Keycloak.
When set, Secret and AdminType fields are ignored.

clientCredentials.description: ClientCredentials configures OAuth2 client credentials grant authentication.

clientId.description:
ClientID is the OAuth2 client ID for authentication.
Can be a direct value or a reference to a key in a Secret or ConfigMap.

configMapKeyRef.description: Selects a key of a ConfigMap.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

secretKeyRef.description: Selects a key of a secret.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

clientSecretRef.description: ClientSecretRef is a reference to a secret key containing the client secret.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

passwordGrant.description: PasswordGrant configures resource owner password grant authentication.

passwordRef.description: PasswordRef is a reference to a secret key containing the password.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

username.description:
Username is the admin username for password grant authentication.
Can be a direct value or a reference to a key in a Secret or ConfigMap.

configMapKeyRef.description: Selects a key of a ConfigMap.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

secretKeyRef.description: Selects a key of a secret.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

x-kubernetes-validations.message: one of passwordGrant or clientCredentials must be set
passwordGrant and clientCredentials are mutually exclusive

caCert.description:
CACert defines the root certificate authority
that api clients use when verifying server certificates.
Resources should be in the namespace defined in operator OPERATOR_NAMESPACE env.

configMapKeyRef.description: Selects a key of a ConfigMap.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

secretKeyRef.description: Selects a key of a secret.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.

insecureSkipVerify.description:
InsecureSkipVerify controls whether api client verifies the server's
certificate chain and host name. If InsecureSkipVerify is true, api client
accepts any certificate presented by the server and any host name in that
certificate.

secret.description:
Secret is a secret name which contains admin credentials.
Deprecated: Use Auth instead. When Auth is set, this field is ignored.

status.description: ClusterKeycloakStatus defines the observed state of ClusterKeycloak.

connected.description: Connected shows if keycloak service is up and running.
